content:
# Matthew Chapter 10 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_010_page_001.jpg, MAT_010_page_002.jpg

1. And having summoned the twelve disciples of him, he gave to them authority over unclean spirits, so as to cast out them, and to heal every disease and every sickness.

2. And of the twelve apostles the names are these: first, Simon the [one] being called Petros, and Andreas the brother of him; and Iakobos the [son] of Zebedaios, and Ioannes the brother of him;

3. Philippos and Bartholomaios; Thomas and Maththaios the tax-collector; Iakobos the [son] of Alphaios, and Thaddaios;

4. Simon the Kananaios, and Ioudas the Iskariotes, the [one] also having betrayed him.

5. These twelve the Yeshua sent out, having commanded them, saying, "Into [a] road of [the] nations do not go, and into [a] city of Samaritans do not enter;

6. but go rather to the sheep, the lost [ones], of [the] house of Israel.

7. And going, proclaim, saying, 'The kingdom of the heavens has drawn near.'

8. Heal [the] sick, raise [the] dead, cleanse lepers, cast out demons. Freely you received, freely give.

9. Do not acquire gold nor silver nor copper into the belts of you,

10. nor [a] bag for the road, nor two tunics, nor sandals, nor [a] staff; for the worker [is] worthy of the food of him.

11. And into whatever city or village you enter, search out who in it is worthy, and there remain until you go out.

12. And entering into the house, greet it.

13. And if indeed the house is worthy, let the peace of you come upon it; but if it is not worthy, let the peace of you return to you.

14. And whoever does not receive you nor hear the words of you, going out of the house or city that, shake off the dust of the feet of you.

15. Truly I say to you, more tolerable it will be for [the] land of Sodom and Gomorra in [the] day of judgment than for the city that.

16. Behold, I send you as sheep in [the] midst of wolves; be therefore wise as the serpents and innocent as the doves.

17. But beware of men; for they will deliver you into councils, and in the synagogues of them they will flog you.

18. And before governors and kings you will be led on account of me, for [a] testimony to them and to the nations.

19. But when they deliver you, do not be anxious how or what you should speak; for it will be given to you in that hour what you should speak.

20. For it is not you the [ones] speaking, but the Spirit of the Father of you, the [one] speaking in you.

21. And brother will deliver brother to death, and [a] father [his] child; and children will rise up against parents and will put to death them.

22. And you will be hated by all on account of the name of me. But the [one] having endured to [the] end, this [one] will be saved.

23. But when they persecute you in this city, flee to the other; for truly I say to you, you will not finish the cities of Israel until the Son of the Man comes.

24. A disciple is not above the teacher, nor [a] slave above the lord of him.

25. [It is] sufficient for the disciple that he become as the teacher of him, and the slave as the lord of him. If the master-of-the-house Beelzeboul they called, how much more the household-members of him.

26. Therefore do not fear them; for nothing is having been hidden which will not be revealed, and secret which will not be known.

27. What I say to you in the darkness, speak in the light; and what into the ear you hear, proclaim upon the housetops.

28. And do not fear the [ones] killing the body, but the soul not being able to kill; but rather fear the [one] being able both soul and body to destroy in Gehenna.

29. Are not two sparrows [sold] for an assarion? And one of them will not fall upon the earth without the Father of you.

30. But of you even the hairs of the head are all having been numbered.

31. Therefore do not fear; you are worth more than many sparrows.

32. Everyone therefore who will confess in me before men, I also will confess in him before the Father of me, the [one] in the heavens.

33. But whoever denies me before men, I also will deny him before the Father of me, the [one] in the heavens.

34. Do not think that I came to bring peace upon the earth; I came not to bring peace but a sword.

35. For I came to set-at-variance a man against the father of him, and a daughter against the mother of her, and a bride against the mother-in-law of her;

36. and enemies of the man [will be] the household-members of him.

37. The [one] loving father or mother above me is not worthy of me; and the [one] loving son or daughter above me is not worthy of me.

38. And whoever does not take the cross of him and follow after me, is not worthy of me.

39. The [one] having found the soul of him will lose it, and the [one] having lost the soul of him on account of me will find it.

40. The [one] receiving you receives me, and the [one] receiving me receives the [one] having sent me.

41. The [one] receiving a prophet in [the] name of a prophet will receive [a] prophet's reward; and the [one] receiving a righteous [one] in [the] name of a righteous [one] will receive [a] righteous [one's] reward.

42. And whoever gives to drink to one of these little [ones] a cup of cold [water] only, in [the] name of a disciple, truly I say to you, he will not lose the reward of him."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Θαδδαῖος (v.3):** Sinaiticus reads "Thaddaios" — some manuscripts read "Lebbaeus" or "Lebbaeus called Thaddaeus." The Sinaiticus reading is the simpler form.

2. **Κανανaῖος (v.4):** "Kananaios" — not "Canaanite" but from Aramaic קנאנא (qan'ana), meaning "zealot." Luke translates this into Greek as ζηλωτής.

3. **Βεελζεβούλ (v.25):** "Beelzeboul" — the Sinaiticus spelling. This name derives from Ba'al Zebul ("Lord of the dwelling/high place"), though popular etymology connected it to Ba'al Zebub ("Lord of flies," 2 Kings 1:2).

4. **ψυχή (vv.28, 39):** Rendered "soul" throughout. This word means both "soul" and "life" — the dual meaning is central to vv.38-39, where losing one's ψυχή (life) for Jesus' sake is paradoxically finding one's ψυχή (true life/soul).

5. **v.23 — "until the Son of the Man comes":** One of the most debated sayings in the Gospels. The Sinaiticus text is clear — ἕως ἂν ἔλθῃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.

— EMET, AI Translator
